description: Tampa Palms Golf & Country Club is an Arthur Hills-designed 18-hole course woven through an Audubon-certified sanctuary of native Florida wetlands north of Tampa. The layout rewards strategic play over power, with tree-lined fairways and natural water features framing nearly every hole. As a private Invited Club property, membership access reflects the exclusivity of the surrounding gated Tampa Palms community.
notes: Tampa Palms is a neighborhood in Tampa, not a separate city.

description: Pelican Golf Club in Belleair is a restored Donald Ross design originally built in 1925 on the grounds of the historic Belleview Biltmore Resort. Beau Welling oversaw a comprehensive renovation completed in 2018, reviving Ross's original contours and adding modern bunker shaping. The course hosts the LPGA Tour's Pelican Women's Championship and stands among Florida's most pedigreed private clubs.
notes: Course is on the site of the old Belleview Biltmore Resort Donald Ross layout; reopened as private club after 2018 Beau Welling renovation.

description: Brooksville Country Club opened in 1971 with a William Mitchell original design, later supplemented by Bobby Weed renovations on the back nine. The semi-private facility sits at 23446 Links Drive and welcomes both members and daily-fee players at accessible rates. It serves as the primary golf anchor in Hernando County's Brooksville community.

Carrollwood Country Club is Tampa's premier private 27-hole facility, originally opened in 1971 with a design by Ed Ault and later renovated by Dan Maples Golf Design. The three nine-hole courses — Meadow, Pine, and Cypress — can be played in any 18-hole combination across Champion Ultradwarf greens. Concert Golf Partners manages the club as an exclusive membership community in northwest Tampa.
notes: City listed as 'Carrollwood' which is a community in Tampa; no city correction needed as Carrollwood is commonly used as a place identifier.

description: The Club at Cheval features a Steve Smyers-designed 18-hole championship course built in 1986 within a gated Lutz community just north of Tampa. The layout blends strategic bunkering with natural Florida terrain, and the club operates as semi-private, welcoming daily-fee play alongside memberships. Heritage Golf Group manages the property as one of the Tampa Bay area's more refined semi-private options.
notes: Listed city 'Cheval' is the gated community name; the actual city is Lutz.

description: The Landings Golf Club of Clearwater, located adjacent to Clearwater Airpark, is a par-63 executive layout with nine par-4s and nine par-3s spread across a compact and walker-friendly 18 holes. Originally opened around 1970, the city-operated facility is one of the most accessible and affordable daily-fee options in Pinellas County. The course suits beginners and seniors while still challenging mid-handicappers with its tight corridors.
notes: Par-63 executive course, 18 holes.

description: Countryside Country Club in Clearwater is a Gary Player-designed 27-hole private facility opened in 1974 and now operated by Invited Clubs. The three nine-hole loops — Lake, Pine, and Bay Head — can be played in various 18-hole combinations across well-maintained Bermuda fairways. The club anchors the Countryside master-planned community with tennis, aquatics, and dining complementing the golf.

Feather Sound Country Club has been one of Tampa Bay's most prestigious private clubs since 1974, featuring a Joe Lee-designed signature course that underwent a major modern renovation in 2022. Set among wetland corridors near the Feather Sound community in Clearwater, the layout rewards precision off the tee. Tour-quality practice facilities and a Har-Tru tennis complex round out a membership experience built for serious golfers.
notes: Joe Lee designed course; major renovation completed 2022.

description: Chi Chi Rodriguez Golf Club is a public par-69 course in Clearwater that opened in 1989 and is closely tied to the Chi Chi Rodriguez Youth Foundation, which uses golf as a vehicle for at-risk youth development. The layout features more than 70 sand traps, water on 12 holes, and two-tiered greens that make it more demanding than its par suggests. The foundation's mission adds distinctive community purpose to what is otherwise a polished daily-fee facility.
notes: Affiliated with Chi Chi Rodriguez Youth Foundation.

description: Diamond Hill Golf Club in Dover opened in the summer of 1959 on land shaped by former phosphate mining operations, giving the course dramatic elevation changes uncommon in Florida. Designed by Chic Adams, the 7,002-yard layout carries a slope of 135 and includes a signature par-3 with an 80-foot drop from tee to green over water. The semi-private club has operated continuously as one of central Florida's most distinctive vintage courses.
notes: Built on former phosphate mine land; unusual elevation changes for Florida.

description: Dunedin Stirling Links is an 18-hole all-par-3 course spread across 27 peaceful acres in Dunedin, a short drive from Clearwater Beach. Built in 1960, the layout is a classic Florida short course that remains accessible to walkers, beginners, and seniors while still testing shot-making through varied yardages and water features. The course operates as a public daily-fee facility and pairs well with the adjacent full-length Dunedin Golf Club.
notes: 18-hole par-3 course.

description: Dunedin Golf Club is one of Florida's most historically significant public courses, a 1927 Donald Ross design that Ross himself called a masterpiece among his works. The club served as the home of the PGA of America from 1945 through 1962 and was listed on the National Register of Historic Places in 2014. A $6.3 million restoration completed in recent years stripped away nearly a century of alterations to reveal the original Ross routing and green complexes.
notes: National Register of Historic Places; former PGA of America headquarters 1945-1962.

description: Pasadena Yacht & Country Club in Gulfport has been one of the Tampa Bay area's most storied private clubs since its founding in 1924, combining an 18-hole championship golf course with a full-service marina. The historic layout has been continuously refined over a century, serving a membership that values both golf and waterfront lifestyle. The club's longevity and dual-sport identity — golf and sailing — give it a character not found at most Florida country clubs.
notes: Founded 1924; combined golf and yacht club.

description: Skyview at Terra Vista is a semi-private 18-hole championship course built in 1999 within the gated Terra Vista community in Hernando, Florida. Stretching 7,142 yards from the back tees, the layout designed by Terry LaGree offers some of the most demanding play in Citrus County, with strategic bunkering and water features throughout. The course is open to outside play and draws golfers from across the Tampa Bay and Nature Coast region.
notes: Located in Hernando, FL (Citrus County), not 'Spring Hill'. Address is 2100 N Terra Vista Blvd, Hernando, FL 34442.
Camp Phosphate Co #1 is a historical phosphate mining operation, not a golf course.

description: Rivard Golf Club in Brooksville is a public 18-hole course opened in 1994 and designed by Lee Duxstad, situated within the Rivard residential community in Hernando County. The par-72 layout plays through natural Florida terrain with fairways bordered by native vegetation and occasional water features. The course offers an affordable and accessible option for golfers across the Hernando County area.
notes: Address: 18001 Rivard Blvd, Brooksville, FL 34604. Listed as 'Hernando County' city.

description: Timber Pines Country Club is a 55+ active adult community in Spring Hill offering four golf courses across 63 total holes, including the 18-hole championship Grand Pines course and two executive layouts. Established in 1982 and 100% member-owned and debt-free, the community's golf is reserved for residents aged 55 and older. The breadth of options — from the championship Grand Pines to a par-3 pitch-and-putt — makes this one of Florida's most golf-centric retirement communities.
notes: 55+ community; golf restricted to residents/members. Four courses total.

description: Temple Terrace Golf & Country Club holds the distinction of being Florida's only 18-hole golf course listed on the National Register of Historic Places, with a design dating to 1922 that predates the city of Temple Terrace itself. The course was central to the founding of the planned community and later became the third Florida golf course added to the National Register in 2012. Now operating as a semi-private facility, it welcomes public tee times alongside club memberships.
notes: City was listed as 'Inverness Ave'; correct city is Temple Terrace. National Register of Historic Places since 2012.

description: East Bay Golf Club in Largo is a public 18-hole facility opened in 1961 and located at 702 Country Club Drive off East Bay Drive. The layout is one of the older continuously operating public courses in Pinellas County and offers straightforward, affordable daily-fee golf accessible to players of all skill levels. A separate par-3 layout on the same property adds a shorter practice option.
notes: Also has a separate par-3 course on property.

description: Cove Cay Golf Club is a semi-private 18-hole course opened in 1972 along the shores of Tampa Bay within the gated Cove Cay community in Clearwater. Designed by David Wallace, the layout features scenic water views and abundant wildlife set against a backdrop of Old Florida terrain. Public daily-fee play is welcome, making it one of the more accessible waterfront golf experiences in the Tampa Bay area.
notes: Address is Clearwater; listed under Largo. Zip 33760.

description: Lutz Executive Golf Center was a 9-hole par-27 short course and driving range facility in Lutz, opened in 2001 at 2220 Newberger Road. The property featured seven lit practice areas with a 275-yard carry range and served as a training and beginner-oriented facility in the north Tampa corridor. The facility is currently listed as closed as of early 2026.
notes: CLOSED as of January 2026 per Yelp. 9-hole par-3 / driving range facility.

description: TPC Tampa Bay is a Bobby Weed-designed 18-hole championship course opened in 1991 as part of the PGA Tour's network of Tournament Players Clubs. The 6,898-yard par-71 layout in Lutz earned a 4.5-star Golf Digest rating and has long been regarded as one of the best upscale public-access courses in the Tampa Bay area. The course's strategic bunkering, generous fairways, and immaculate conditioning set the standard for daily-fee golf in the region.
notes: Cover image URL is an estimate based on TPC website structure; verify before use.

description: Northdale Golf & Tennis Club is a public 18-hole facility in Tampa opened in 1977 and located at 4417 Northdale Boulevard, approximately 12 miles north of Tampa International Airport. The accessible daily-fee course serves the northwest Tampa residential corridor and welcomes golfers of all skill levels without a membership requirement. Tennis courts complement the golf offering at this longstanding community facility.
notes: Northdale is a Tampa community/neighborhood.

description: The Eagles Golf Club in Odessa has anchored public golf in the Tampa market since the Lakes Course opened in 1970, with the Forest Course added in 1995 to create a 36-hole complex. The two 18-hole layouts offer distinct experiences — the Lakes emphasizing water strategy, the Forest playing through mature trees — and together represent the standard for upscale public access golf on Tampa's west side. The club is widely considered among the best value propositions for daily-fee play in the region.
notes: 36 holes total: Lakes Course (1970) and Forest Course (1995).

description: Lansbrook Golf Club is a public 18-hole course in Palm Harbor that has served the community since 1975 from its location at 4605 Village Center Drive. The layout offers a complete practice facility including driving range, chipping area, and putting green alongside accessible daily-fee play. Situated in one of Pinellas County's more established residential communities, the course draws a loyal local following.

Innisbrook Golf Resort in Palm Harbor is one of Florida's premier golf destinations, home to four championship courses including the Copperhead — host of the PGA Tour's Valspar Championship — and three supporting layouts designed by Lawrence Packard when the resort opened in 1970. Set on 900 acres of rolling terrain unusual for Florida, the Copperhead's tree-lined corridors and dramatic elevation changes have challenged Tour professionals for decades. Now branded as a Salamander Golf & Spa Resort, Innisbrook blends tournament-caliber golf with full resort accommodations.
notes: Four courses: Copperhead (PGA Tour Valspar Championship host), Island, North, South. Resort property.

description: Lexington Oaks Golf Club opened in January 2000 as a daily-fee public course in Wesley Chapel, just minutes from I-75 at 26133 Lexington Oaks Boulevard. The 18-hole layout integrates water features and native Florida landscaping throughout a community-adjacent design that remains accessible and affordable for north Tampa–area golfers. The course serves the fast-growing Wesley Chapel market without a membership requirement.
notes: Listed city was 'Pasco County'; correct city is Wesley Chapel.

description: Mainlands Golf Course in Pinellas Park is a public 18-hole layout that has served the Pinellas County community since approximately 1970 from its address at 9445 Mainlands Boulevard West. The accessible, ADA-compliant facility is one of the most affordable daily-fee options in the area and caters to a broad range of skill levels. The course is wheelchair accessible with compliant parking and entrance.

Timber Greens Country Club is a semi-private 18-hole course designed by Ron Garl and opened in 1995 within a 55+ active adult community in New Port Richey. The par-71 layout plays 6,133 yards over Bermuda fairways and Paspalum greens, offering a refined retirement-community golf experience open to outside public play. Timber Greens is regarded as one of the more polished golf retirement communities along Florida's Nature Coast.
notes: 55+ community; semi-private, public play welcome. Ron Garl design. Located in New Port Richey, not 'River Ridge'.

description: River Ridge Golf Club in New Port Richey is a public 18-hole course built in 1985 at 11022 Tee Time Circle, offering straightforward daily-fee access in western Pasco County. The layout plays through a residential community setting with natural Florida vegetation lining the fairways. The course is a reliable and affordable option for golfers in the New Port Richey–Port Richey corridor.
notes: Located in New Port Richey, not 'River Ridge'.

description: Cypress Creek Golf Club in Ruskin is a semi-private 18-hole course that opened in January 1989, designed by Dean Refram and Steven Smyers on 640 acres of protected oak and cypress forest preserve in the SouthShore area south of Tampa. The par-72 layout is open to public daily-fee play seven days a week alongside membership options, and the forested corridors give it an unusual sense of seclusion for a Tampa Bay-area course. Smyers went on to become one of Florida's most respected architects, making this an early example of his work.
notes: Listed as 'Cypress Course' in input; confirmed as Cypress Creek Golf Club at 1011 Cypress Village Blvd, Ruskin FL 33573.

description: St. Petersburg Country Club traces its roots to 1924 when it opened as Lakewood Country Club, making it one of the oldest continuously operating private clubs in the Tampa Bay region. The member-owned and operated 18-hole course at 2000 Country Club Way South is not open to public play and offers a traditional private club experience with golf, tennis, and dining. A century of operation has shaped a culture and layout that reflects the city's golf heritage.
notes: Originally opened as Lakewood Country Club in 1924.

description: Twin Brooks Golf Course is a public 9-hole par-3 facility in St. Petersburg that has operated since 1957 at 3800 22nd Avenue South. One of the city's municipal golf options, the course is ideal for beginners, juniors, and players looking for a quick round without the commitment of a full 18-hole layout. A driving range and PGA instruction complement the short course, making Twin Brooks a genuine community golf resource.
notes: 9-hole par-3 with driving range. Municipal facility.

description: Isla Del Sol Yacht & Country Club occupies a private island between St. Pete Beach and downtown St. Petersburg, with an 18-hole golf course that opened in 1977 alongside a deep-water marina. The course overlooks Boca Ciega Bay and the intracoastal waterway, offering members a setting that is as much about lifestyle as it is about golf. Nine clay tennis courts, a private pool, and first-class dining complete a members-only club environment defined by its island exclusivity.
notes: Private island setting on Boca Ciega Bay.

description: Bay Pointe Golf Course is a semi-private 18-hole course in Seminole built in 1968 at 9399 Commodore Drive, making it one of the older continuously operating layouts in Pinellas County. The compact par layout plays under 3,200 yards and offers an accessible, affordable option for golfers of varying skill levels. Its proximity to the Gulf beaches and reasonable daily fees make Bay Pointe a popular choice for local residents and visitors.

Bardmoor Golf & Tennis Club in Seminole opened in 1971 under the direction of founder Oliver Bardes and has operated as a public-access facility for more than five decades. The 18-hole par-72 course features Tif-Eagle greens and sits within minutes of Florida's Gulf beaches, drawing both residents and visiting golfers year-round. Tennis courts complement the golf and make Bardmoor one of the more complete athletic facilities in the Pinellas County area.

Seven Hills Golfers Club is a public 18-hole course in Spring Hill that has welcomed daily-fee play since 1989 at 10599 Fairchild Road. The par-72 layout stretches 6,715 yards and offers one of the more challenging public options in Hernando County without requiring a membership. The course serves the large residential Spring Hill community and is accessible seven days a week at public rates.

High Point Golf Club in Brooksville is a member-owned, not-for-profit 501(c)(7) private club established in 1976 within the High Point 55+ residential community. The 18-hole par-72 course is reserved for members and their guests, with tee times not required — reflecting the relaxed, resident-focused ethos of the community. Golf and social memberships are available to residents of the surrounding High Point neighborhood.
notes: 55+ community; private members-only. Located in Brooksville, not Spring Hill.

description: Mangrove Bay Municipal Golf Course is a city-operated par-72 championship course in St. Petersburg that has served the public since 1977 from its 180-acre site at 875 62nd Avenue NE. The layout sits along Tampa Bay's northeast corridor with water features and native vegetation defining the character of the course. As one of the City of St. Petersburg's two municipal courses, Mangrove Bay offers reliable, affordable public golf in a scenic natural setting.
notes: City-operated municipal course.

description: Scepter Golf Club is the newest of Sun City Center's three golf facilities, opened in 2005 and designed by Florida architect Chip Powell with five sets of tee decks to accommodate the 55+ active adult residents it serves. The course is part of the ClubLink portfolio, giving members access to more than 50 championship clubs. As a 55+ retirement community course, Scepter operates primarily as a private facility for residents, with limited public access during off-peak periods.
notes: 55+ retirement community course. Part of ClubLink network. Limited public play off-season.

description: Club Renaissance at Sun City Center is a semi-private par-72 course that opened in 2002 within the broader Sun City Center 55+ active adult community, stretching from 3,982 to 6,701 yards across seven sets of tee decks. The Mediterranean-style clubhouse features a golf shop, pool, spa, fitness center, and restaurant that complement the golf experience for residents and visitors alike. Public play is welcome, making this one of the more accessible options within the Sun City Center retirement community.
notes: 55+ community course; semi-private with public tee times available.

description: Caloosa Golf & Country Club is a member-owned, not-for-profit private club in Sun City Center that has served the 55+ active adult community since 1978. Play is strictly limited to members aged 30 and older — one of the few clubs in the area with a minimum age requirement below the community's 55+ standard — and the club operates debt-free on resident membership dues. The course is a foundational amenity of the Sun City Center retirement community.
notes: 55+ community; private members-only, minimum age 30. Member-owned non-profit.

description: Freedom Fairways is the only executive golf course in Sun City Center, a 9-par-3 and 9-par-4 layout opened in 1992 on the grounds of Freedom Plaza, one of the area's premier 55+ retirement communities. The course is open to the public seven days a week with membership options available, making it the most accessible golf facility within the Sun City Center complex. Its shorter format suits the active retirement demographic it primarily serves.
notes: 55+ community context; executive course (par-3s and par-4s only). Public access.

description: The Claw at USF was a public 18-hole course on the University of South Florida campus in Tampa, designed by William F. Mitchell and opened in 1967. The par-71 layout served the university and surrounding community for over five decades before USF closed public golf operations in September 2023 due to ongoing financial losses. The 138-acre site has since been approved for redevelopment into a mixed-use 'Fletcher District' including student housing, retail, and a hotel.
notes: CLOSED September 2023. Land approved for redevelopment as 'Fletcher District'.

description: Westchase Golf Club is a premium daily-fee public course in Tampa designed by Lloyd Clifton and opened in 1992 at 11602 Westchase Golf Drive. The 6,699-yard par-72 layout anchors one of Tampa's most desirable master-planned communities and is consistently rated among the best value options for public golf in the region. Clifton's design makes strategic use of natural Florida wetlands and conservation corridors throughout the course.

Avila Golf & Country Club in Tampa is one of the most exclusive private clubs in the Southeast, operating on a private invitation-only membership model requiring sponsorship by two current members. The course was originally designed in 1980 and comprehensively redesigned by Jack Nicklaus in 1988, resulting in an 18-hole par-71 championship layout stretching 6,926 yards through the gated Avila community in north Tampa. The Nicklaus design signature is evident throughout in the strategic bunkering and green complexes.
notes: Jack Nicklaus redesign 1988. Invitation-only private membership.

description: Countryway Golf Club is a public 18-hole facility in western Tampa that has welcomed daily-fee play since opening in approximately 1989 at 11111 West Waters Avenue. The course serves the Countryway residential community and surrounding west Tampa area with accessible green fees and no membership requirement. The layout integrates natural Florida wetlands and vegetation to create defined corridors on a relatively flat but strategic layout.

Babe Zaharias Golf Course has operated as a Tampa municipal course since 1974, when the City of Tampa renamed the historic Forest Hills Golf and Country Club — originally established in 1926 — in honor of the legendary multi-sport athlete. The public course at 11412 Forest Hills Drive is part of Tampa's municipal golf system and offers accessible daily-fee play in north Tampa. The course's history as one of the city's original country clubs gives it a heritage that distinguishes it from newer municipal layouts.
notes: Originally Forest Hills Golf and Country Club (1926); renamed Babe Zaharias by City of Tampa in 1974.

description: Hunter's Green Country Club is New Tampa's most exclusive private club, anchored by a Tom Fazio-designed 18-hole course opened in 1989 and renovated in 2006 within a gated community at 18101 Longwater Run Drive. Fazio's layout blends strategic water features with rolling fairways and elevated green complexes in a design that consistently ranks among the top private courses in the Tampa Bay area. Operated by Invited Clubs, the facility offers cross-bay reciprocal access to affiliated clubs across the region.
notes: Tom Fazio design 1989, renovated 2006. Invited Clubs property.

description: Heritage Isles Golf and Country Club is a public 18-hole course in New Tampa opened in 2000, designed by Gordon G. Lewis and Jed Azinger to preserve the natural character of the land while creating a demanding layout that stretches 6,976 yards with a 132 slope rating. Water cuts through native cypress heads and wetland corridors throughout the course, making it one of the more ecologically distinctive public facilities in the Bay area. Despite the championship-level challenge, the course remains open to daily-fee play without a membership.

notes: Ambiguous entry — 'North Course' in Tampa cannot be confirmed as a specific course without more information.

description: Rocky Point Golf Course is one of Tampa's most storied public courses, with origins tracing to 1911 when the Tampa Automobile Club laid out the original links on a peninsula jutting into Tampa Bay. The City of Tampa assumed control in 1953 and reopened the course in June 1954, with subsequent improvements cementing its reputation as a scenic and accessible public facility. The waterfront location along Dana Shores Drive and Tampa Bay views make it one of the more distinctive settings for a municipal course in the region.
notes: Original 1911 Tampa Automobile Club layout; reopened as municipal course in 1954. Tampa Sports Authority managed.

description: Tarpon Springs Golf Course is one of Florida's oldest continuously operating public courses, with the original nine holes dating to 1907 and an 18-hole expansion completed in 1927 by Wayne Styles and John Van Kleek. Owned and operated by the City of Tarpon Springs, the course at 1310 South Pinellas Avenue is part of the Florida Historic Golf Trail and serves the community as a municipal facility. Its longevity and connection to early Florida resort golf give it a historical significance matched by few courses in the state.
notes: Part of Florida Historic Golf Trail. One of oldest courses in FL (1907 original 9 holes).

description: Heritage Springs Country Club is a 55+ private gated community in Trinity, designed by Ron Garl and opened in 2000 at the end of Robert Trent Jones Parkway. Golf membership is bundled with home ownership in Heritage Springs, giving every resident access to the club without a separate initiation fee — a model that reinforces the residential identity of the course. The 24/7 guarded entrance and resort-style amenities including a pool, fitness center, and theater make this one of Trinity's most complete active adult communities.
notes: 55+ community; membership included with home ownership. Ron Garl design.

description: Fox Hollow Golf Club in Trinity opened in 1994 as a semi-private course off Robert Trent Jones Parkway, offering 18 holes of daily-fee play alongside membership options within one of Pasco County's most active golf corridors. The course benefits from proximity to Heritage Springs and the broader Trinity residential market, drawing a consistent mix of members and daily-fee players. Fox Hollow's layout integrates water features and mature vegetation throughout a well-maintained par-71 design.

River Hills Country Club in Valrico is a private gated community club established in 1989 at 3943 New River Hills Parkway, offering 18 holes of championship golf to members and a limited 'Member for a Day' visitor option. The course winds through the rolling terrain of east Hillsborough County with water features and mature tree corridors defining the layout. As a private club, River Hills is valued for its exclusivity and the residential community lifestyle it anchors.

Saddlebrook Resort in Wesley Chapel has offered championship golf since 1981 on 480 acres of rolling terrain unusual for the Florida flatlands, with two courses originally designed by Arnold Palmer — the Oak Course, recently redesigned by Rees Jones, and the Pine/Cypress combination layout. The resort's 27 holes, luxury hotel, full spa, and renowned tennis academy position Saddlebrook as one of Tampa Bay's most complete destination resorts. The Oak Course debuted its Rees Jones redesign in late 2025.
notes: Arnold Palmer original design; Rees Jones redesign of Oak Course completed 2025. Resort property.

description: Ventura Country Club in Orlando is a semi-private 18-hole course designed by Mark Mahannah and opened in 1980 within a gated residential community at 3333 Woodgate Boulevard. The course welcomes outside daily-fee play alongside memberships and serves the east Orlando residential market as a well-maintained community golf option. The gated neighborhood setting and semi-private model make Ventura accessible to visiting golfers while preserving a member-oriented atmosphere.
notes: City was listed as 'Woodgate Blvd'; correct city is Orlando. Located in east Orlando, zip 32822.
